title: Project document
generated_summary: Climate change is projected to have significant impacts on water resources in an already arid Turkmenistan. The Government of Turkmanistan is proposing to take a comprehensive approach to water adaptation in the agricultural sector. This initiative aims to increase resilience in 3 different agro - climatic zones in Turkmenism by implementing hard water efficiency and irrigation measures. It focuses on increasing the resilience of water resources for the most vulnerable and water - stressed communities.

title: Inception report
generated_summary: Mauritius has secured a grant of USD 9, 119, 240 from the Adaptation Fund for the implementation of the project " Climate Change Adaptation Programme in the Coastal Zone of Mauritius " over a duration of five years. The inception workshop was held on the 30th August 2012 at Hennessy Park Hotel, Ebene to officially launch the project. The afternoon session involved a more in - depth technical discussions and presentations with working groups.

title: Project completion report
generated_summary: The Republic of Mauritius secured a grant of USD 9, 119, 240 in 2012 from the Adaptation Fund Board for implementation of the project. The objective is to increase climate resilience of communities and livelihoods in coastal areas.
